findings.description: The remote SSH server is vulnerable to a prefix truncation attack known as 'Terrapin'. This attack allows a man-in-the-middle attacker to strip a number of messages at the beginning of the SSH handshake, potentially downgrading security.
findings.plugin_output:
The following supported encryption algorithms allow the attack:
  - chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com
  - *-etm@openssh.com MACs

KEX Algorithms: curve25519-sha256
Server version: SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_8.9p1 Ubuntu-3ubuntu0.6
findings.solution: Upgrade OpenSSH to 9.6 or later which disables the affected algorithms by default. Alternatively, remove chacha20-poly1305 and *-etm MACs from sshd_config.

findings.description: The version of OpenSSH installed on the remote host is prior to 9.8. It is, therefore, affected by a signal handler race condition in the server (sshd). If a client does not authenticate within the LoginGraceTime (120 seconds by default), then sshd's SIGALRM handler is called asynchronously.
findings.plugin_output:
Installed version: 8.9p1
Fixed version: 9.8p1

Note: LoginGraceTime is set to the default of 120 seconds. Temporary mitigation: Set LoginGraceTime 0

findings.description: The server's X.509 certificate cannot be trusted. The certificate chain was signed by an unknown certificate authority.
findings.plugin_output:
Subject: CN=web-prod-01.corp.internal
Issuer: CN=Corp Internal CA, O=Corp
Not Before: 2025-01-15
Not After: 2025-01-15
Error: Certificate expired on 2025-01-15
findings.solution: Renew the SSL/TLS certificate. Ensure certificate authority is trusted by clients.

findings.description: The remote host allows SSL/TLS connections with one or more Diffie-Hellman moduli less than or equal to 1024 bits. An attacker could decrypt TLS connections via a downgrade attack (aka Logjam).
findings.plugin_output:
Weak DH group detected:
  Modulus size: 1024 bits
  Cipher suite: DHE-RSA-AES256-SHA
findings.solution: Reconfigure the service to use a Diffie-Hellman moduli of 2048 bits or greater. Disable export cipher suites.

findings.description: The version of PostgreSQL installed on the remote host is prior to 16.2, 15.6, 14.11, 13.14, or 12.18 and is affected by a vulnerability: A user having MAINTAIN privilege on a relation can trigger execution of arbitrary SQL functions under the identity of the relation owner.
findings.plugin_output:
Installed version: PostgreSQL 14.9
Fixed version: PostgreSQL 14.11

findings.description: The PostgreSQL server running on the remote host allows connections without authentication using the 'trust' authentication method for some databases.
findings.plugin_output:
Database 'analytics' accepts connections with 'trust' authentication from 0.0.0.0/0
pg_hba.conf entry: host all all 0.0.0.0/0 trust
findings.solution: Modify pg_hba.conf to require password authentication (md5 or scram-sha-256) for all connections. Remove trust authentication for external connections.

findings.description: The remote NTP server responds to mode 6 queries. Devices that respond to these queries have the potential to be used in NTP amplification attacks.
findings.solution: Restrict mode 6 queries by adding 'restrict default noquery' to ntp.conf.

findings.description: The Jenkins server installed on the remote host is prior to 2.442 or LTS 2.426.3 and is vulnerable to arbitrary file read via the CLI endpoint (CVE-2024-23897, CVSS 9.8). An unauthenticated attacker can read arbitrary files including private SSH keys, credentials.xml, and secrets.
findings.plugin_output:
Installed version: Jenkins 2.387.3 LTS
Fixed version: Jenkins LTS 2.426.3

Proof of concept:
CLI command: java -jar jenkins-cli.jar -s http://10.0.3.22:8080/ help '@/etc/passwd'
Result: returns /etc/passwd contents
findings.solution: Update Jenkins to 2.442 or LTS 2.426.3 or later. Disable CLI access if not needed: JENKINS_URL/manage/configureSecurity/ -> Disable CLI over Remoting.

findings.description: Java 8u292 (EOL) detected. Java 8 reached end of public updates in 2019.
findings.solution: Upgrade to Java 17 LTS or Java 21 LTS.

findings.plugin_name: Redis Server Unprotected by Password Authentication
findings.description: The remote Redis server does not require authentication. An unauthenticated, remote attacker can access the database and potentially execute OS commands via the SLAVEOF or CONFIG SET commands.
findings.plugin_output:
Successful unauthenticated connection:
127.0.0.1:6379> PING
+PONG
127.0.0.1:6379> INFO server
# Server
redis_version:7.0.14
...
Keys found: 14,847
Sample key types: session:*, payment:*, user:*
findings.solution: Set requirepass in redis.conf. Enable protected-mode. Bind Redis to 127.0.0.1 or VPC-internal IPs only. Disable dangerous commands: CONFIG, SLAVEOF, DEBUG.

findings.plugin_name: Redis Server Accessible from Untrusted Network
findings.description: Redis is listening on 0.0.0.0:6379 and is accessible from external networks.
findings.solution: Change Redis bind address to 127.0.0.1 or a specific internal IP. Apply network-level controls via firewall.
